title: A Poem About What Happens to People Who Stay Real
description:
This poem came from a quiet moment — watching authenticity get punished while performance gets rewarded. Not a rant. Just a name for something most of us feel but rarely say directly.

In this video I read the poem and share what's behind it: what it does to creators specifically, why the masked world goes after genuine people deliberately, and what the waking up at the end actually means.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

I write about the honest parts of building a creative life — the discipline, the doubt, the moments that don't make good content but make good work. New videos every week across poetry, data science, and self-development.

[LINKS_PLACEHOLDER]
thumbnail_concept: Close-up of a cracked white porcelain mask resting on dark concrete, single overhead light source casting hard shadow, text overlay in thin cream serif font: 'They weren't ready for real' — muted charcoal and bone color palette, no bright accents
end_screen_suggestion: Surface the Breath of Poetry playlist or the most recent life and self-development spoken word video
